classifications.phrase: decided to lower the cash rate by 25 basis points
to support the economy as it responds to the global coronavirus outbreak
global growth in the first half of 2020 will be lower than earlier expected
GDP growth in the March quarter is likely to be noticeably weaker than earlier expected
A gradual lift in wages growth would be a welcome development and is needed for…
expected to delay progress in Australia towards full employment and the inflation target
appropriate to ease monetary policy further to provide additional support to employment
prepared to ease monetary policy further to support the Australian economy

payload.rationale: The statement signals a dovish stance dominated by growth and employment concerns driven by coronavirus uncertainty, with limited hawkish signals on inflation requiring future wage growth.
